message:
refactor(tests): clarify control lane policy invariant semantics and test naming

The control lane conformance tests used language implying a global dequeue
ordering guarantee ("scheduled before"), but the LaneScheduler API only
guarantees lane isolation and admission — not cross-lane scheduling order.

- Rename cancel_lane_tasks_scheduled_before_ready_under_mixed_load →
  cancel_lane_tasks_remain_admissible_under_ready_load
- Rename cancel_lane_not_starved_under_heavy_ready_load →
  cancel_lane_remains_admissible_under_heavy_non_cancel_load
- Update INV-CLP-CANCEL-BEFORE-READY and INV-CLP-CANCEL-NEVER-STARVED
  doc comments to describe admissibility rather than ordering
- Clarify test comments to note that conformance checks lane isolation
  plus successful admission, not global dequeue order

Also updates beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1z5a.15","title":"bd-1z5a support: backfill real rch build IDs into operator E2E artifacts","description":"Why this exists:
The bd-1z5a operator E2E bundle currently records stage commands, exit codes, and event-family evidence, but the machine-readable build provenance is still weak: operator_e2e_bundle.json and operator_e2e_log.jsonl carry null build_id values, and the operator checker only requires summary.build_ids to be a list instead of proving real offloaded build ids were captured. That leaves a truthful functional bundle but an avoidable provenance gap for acceptance criteria 5, 6, and 8.

What this task must do:
- recover the exact rch build ids for the existing successful operator E2E stage commands where possible,
- update the operator harness/checker/tests/artifacts so the bundle fails closed if build provenance silently drops back to null on stages that came from rch lanes,
- keep scope on the operator harness/checker/artifact files and stay off the core verifier Rust files plus NavyRiver's replacement-gap evidence-pack lane.

Acceptance criteria:
1. operator_e2e_bundle.json and operator_e2e_log.jsonl carry concrete build ids for the rch-backed stage entries instead of all-null provenance.
2. operator_e2e_summary.json / .md surface those recovered build ids consistently.
3. scripts/check_verifier_replay_operator_e2e.py and its unit tests fail closed if the required stage build provenance disappears again.
4. Scope stays on tests/e2e, scripts/tests, and operator E2E artifacts only.","notes":"Prepared support lane: telemetry provenance rows recovered and posted in bd-1z5a thread; no edits landed because NavyRiver holds wildcard reservation on operator_e2e artifact files. Partial file reservations were released so this bead no longer blocks other agents while awaiting artifact-owner coordination.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftBeaver","created_at":"2026-03-11T10:22:30.291875667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:58:20.868443428Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T05:58:20.868413562Z","close_reason":"Operator E2E build provenance already backfilled; checker now fails closed on Markdown provenance drift and targeted unit regressions cover build-id/provenance loss.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["replacement-critical","self-contained","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.15","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T10:22:30.291875667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1z5a.17","title":"[bd-1z5a support] Reject zero-length replay capsule freshness windows","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/verifier_economy/mod.rs::replay_capsule_freshness_window_valid` currently accepts `issued_at == expires_at` because it allows a freshness window of `0..=3600` seconds and only checks `now < expires_at`. That means a replay capsule with no positive validity interval is treated as fresh if the shared timestamp is still in the future.

What this bug must do:
- make replay capsule freshness fail closed for zero-length windows
- keep the check deterministic and aligned with existing expiry behavior
- add focused inline regressions for zero-length and positive-length cases

Acceptance criteria:
1. `replay_capsule_freshness_window_valid` rejects `expires_at <= issued_at`.
2. `VerifierEconomyRegistry::verify_capsule_integrity` rejects zero-length freshness windows with `FreshnessWindowInvalid`.
3. Inline tests cover the zero-length window rejection and preserve a positive future window success case.
4. Cargo-heavy validation uses `rch` only.","notes":"Robot triage update:
- `bv --recipe actionable --robot-plan` still ranks `bd-1z5a` as highest-impact track
- actionable tracks shown: `bd-1z5a`, `bd-1z5a.17`, `bd-24h7`, `bd-24h7.21`
- non-overlapping actionable lane already assigned to me remains `bd-1z5a.17`, so continuing this shard to closeout instead of claiming a new overlapping bead

Validation status remains feature-aware because `verifier_economy` is gated behind `extended-surfaces`. Latest follow-up fix requires `issued_at <= now < expires_at`.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonOtter","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:30:11.652018788Z","created_by":"CrimsonOtter","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:06:37.764507456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:30:11.652018788Z","created_by":"CrimsonOtter","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1z5a.18","title":"[bd-1z5a support] Reject malformed expected_output_hash in universal verifier capsules","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/connector/universal_verifier_sdk.rs::validate_manifest` only checks `expected_output_hash` for non-emptiness. A capsule signed by a trusted key but carrying a nonsense expected hash like `wrong_hash` is therefore treated as an ordinary replay divergence instead of an invalid capsule. That blurs malformed-capsule rejection with genuine replay mismatch and weakens the expected-result commitment surface in the highest-impact verifier lane.

What this task must do:
- require `expected_output_hash` to be a real sha256-shaped digest during manifest validation
- preserve the ability for valid-but-wrong digests to yield replay `Fail` verdicts
- add focused tests for malformed-hash rejection and valid-shaped divergence

Acceptance criteria:
1. `validate_manifest` rejects malformed `expected_output_hash` values instead of accepting any non-empty string.
2. `replay_capsule` rejects malformed expected hashes before replay/signature-verdict evaluation.
3. A valid-shaped but wrong expected hash still produces a deterministic `Fail` verdict, not a manifest error.
4. Cargo-heavy validation uses `rch` only and stays scoped to the universal verifier SDK target.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BronzeCliff","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:45.180895461Z","created_by":"BronzeCliff","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:45.180895461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z5a.18","depends_on_id":"bd-1z5a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:09:45.180895461Z","created_by":"BronzeCliff","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

No code changes required in this support slice; target file already matched bd-q92s patch description.","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:34:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qlc6","title":"[10.14] Map remote/control tasks to lane-aware scheduler classes with priority policies.","description":"## Why This Exists

The 9J enhancement maps require that all control-plane and remote-effect workloads run under explicit scheduling discipline to prevent priority inversion, lane starvation, and uncontrolled resource consumption. Without lane-aware scheduling, a flood of low-priority remote tasks can starve critical epoch transitions or marker writes, violating runtime invariant #7 (epoch barriers must complete within bounded time). This bead establishes the foundational scheduler classification that all downstream lane-policy consumers (10.15 lane mapping, 10.11 product integration) depend on. It directly supports invariant #9 (deterministic verification gates) by making task classification auditable and telemetry-backed.

## What This Must Do

1. Define a `SchedulerLane` enum covering at minimum: `ControlCritical`, `RemoteEffect`, `Maintenance`, `Background`, each with configurable priority weight and concurrency cap.
2. Implement a `LaneMappingPolicy` that maps incoming task types (identified by `TaskClass` discriminant) to scheduler lanes based on declarative policy configuration.
3. Enforce lane starvation detection: if any lane has zero completions within a configurable time window while tasks are queued, emit a `LANE_STARVED` alert event with lane ID, queue depth, and elapsed time.
4. Enforce misclassification detection: reject tasks whose `TaskClass` does not match any policy rule and emit `LANE_MISCLASS` event with task metadata.
5. Expose lane telemetry counters: `lane_active_count`, `lane_queued_count`, `lane_completed_total`, `lane_starvation_events`, per-lane latency histograms.
6. Produce a spec document defining the lane model, priority semantics, starvation thresholds, and configuration schema.
7. Produce a conformance test that validates lane assignment correctness, starvation detection firing, and misclassification rejection under controlled load.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Task classes are mapped to lanes by policy; lane starvation and misclassification checks are enforced; lane telemetry is exposed.
- Given 4+ task classes, each maps to exactly one lane per policy; no task class maps to zero lanes.
- Starvation detection fires within 2x the configured starvation window when a lane is blocked.
- Misclassification rejection returns a typed error with the unrecognized `TaskClass` value.
- Telemetry counters increment correctly under concurrent load (verified via test harness).
- Policy changes take effect without restart (hot-reload path exists and is tested).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: lane assignment for each task class; starvation timer triggers on blocked lane; misclassification rejection for unknown task class; policy reload applies new mapping.
- Integration tests: concurrent multi-lane load with starvation injection; lane fairness under skewed workloads; telemetry accuracy under >1000 tasks.
- Conformance tests: `tests/conformance/lane_mapping_enforcement.rs` -- normative test covering all policy rules and edge cases.
- Structured logs: `LANE_ASSIGN` (task_id, task_class, lane_id), `LANE_STARVED` (lane_id, queue_depth, elapsed_ms), `LANE_MISCLASS` (task_class, rejection_reason), `LANE_METRICS` (periodic telemetry snapshot). All events carry trace correlation IDs.

## Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/lane_mapping_policy.md` -- specification of lane model, priority semantics, configuration schema
- `tests/conformance/lane_mapping_enforcement.rs` -- normative conformance tests
- `artifacts/10.14/lane_mapping_metrics.csv` -- telemetry snapshot from conformance run
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-qlc6/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-qlc6/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

- Upstream: bd-v4l0 (global remote bulkhead with `remote_max_in_flight`) -- provides the concurrency envelope this bead subdivides into lanes.
- Downstream: bd-3epz (section-wide verification gate), bd-cuut (10.15 lane mapping for control-plane workloads), bd-lus (10.11 product integration of scheduler lanes), bd-5rh (section gate).","acceptance_criteria":"Task classes are mapped to lanes by policy; lane starvation and misclassification checks are enforced; lane telemetry is exposed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:58.061700429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:44:37.220499422Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:44:37.220471490Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlc6","depends_on_id":"bd-v4l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:15.698361701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-qlc6.1","title":"[bd-qlc6] Detect queued-lane starvation before first completion","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` only reports starvation when `last_completion_ms` is `Some(...)`. That means a lane that immediately saturates, queues additional work, and then stays blocked past the starvation window is treated as non-starved forever until the first completion happens. `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs::starvation_detected_when_lane_idle_too_long` expects the opposite behavior: queued Background work should be reported as starved after the window elapses even if the lane has never completed a task yet.

What this bug must do:
- make starvation detection use a stable baseline for lanes with queued work but no completion history yet
- keep the implementation fail-closed and deterministic
- update inline/unit/conformance coverage so the never-completed queued case is treated as starved after the configured window instead of silently ignored

Acceptance criteria:
1. `LaneScheduler::check_starvation` reports starvation for a queued lane after the configured window even if the lane has never completed a task.
2. The scheduler records accurate starvation telemetry and audit evidence for that case.
3. Inline tests in `runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` and the existing conformance expectation in `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs` agree on the same behavior.
4. Validation uses `rch` for cargo-heavy checks only.","notes":"Implemented in `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs`:\n- added `first_queued_at_ms` to track when queued work began waiting\n- starvation now uses the queued-work baseline even before any completion history exists\n- completion resets the queued baseline when work remains queued\n- inline tests now cover pre-window no-starvation, queued-before-first-completion starvation, and audit evidence\n\nVerification status:\n- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo check --all-targets` PASS for `frankenengine-node`; dependency warnings remain in `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/control_plane_mock_inventory.rs`\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` blocked by pre-existing unrelated lints in `crates/franken-node/src/connector/canonical_serializer.rs` and `crates/franken-node/src/connector/crdt.rs`\n- targeted `rch` cargo test lanes are still running/stalling in the shared worker fleet; no green test verdict captured yet

2026-03-12 CrimsonOtter closeout attempt:
- reserved only `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs`, `crates/franken-node/tests/control_lane_policy.rs`, and `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs`
- confirmed no additional source regression in those files during read-only review
- retried remote verification with both wrapper-style and simplified `rch` lanes
- representative builds: `29750734287274374` / `29750734287274375` (vmi1264463) and `29750734287274382` (vmi1227854) all reached execute/compile and then degraded into `progress_stale` without a trustworthy green verdict
- no new source failure surfaced; blocker remains `rch` evidence quality, not scheduler/conformance correctness
- no code edits were made in this closeout attempt; releasing the reservation and returning the bead to `open`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BronzeCliff","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:52:22.105655019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:55:01.046584204Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T20:55:01.046553557Z","close_reason":"Validated existing HEAD starvation fix with green rch inline+conformance tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","scheduler","self-contained"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlc6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-qlc6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T22:52:22.105655019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-qlc6.1","author":"FrostyPond","text":"Closeout pass on 2026-03-12: added Cargo-visible wrapper crates/franken-node/tests/control_lane_policy.rs for existing root conformance spec tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs. First remote conformance run via rch (build 29750734287274312 on vmi1293453) no longer failed with missing test target; instead it surfaced real Rust compile errors in the conformance spec: unused LaneMappingPolicy import and E0502 immutable audit_log() borrows held across sched.complete_task() calls. Fixed those in tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs, then validated file-locally with rustfmt --edition 2024 tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs crates/franken-node/tests/control_lane_policy.rs and git diff --check -- tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs crates/franken-node/tests/control_lane_policy.rs. Second remote rerun via rch (build 29750734287274319 on vmi1156319) progressed through compile but degraded into the shared fleet stall pattern; after several minutes with no new progress it was canceled explicitly to avoid wasting slots. +{"id":"bd-qlc6.1","title":"[bd-qlc6] Detect queued-lane starvation before first completion","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` only reports starvation when `last_completion_ms` is `Some(...)`. That means a lane that immediately saturates, queues additional work, and then stays blocked past the starvation window is treated as non-starved forever until the first completion happens. `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs::starvation_detected_when_lane_idle_too_long` expects the opposite behavior: queued Background work should be reported as starved after the window elapses even if the lane has never completed a task yet.

What this bug must do:
- make starvation detection use a stable baseline for lanes with queued work but no completion history yet
- keep the implementation fail-closed and deterministic
- update inline/unit/conformance coverage so the never-completed queued case is treated as starved after the configured window instead of silently ignored

Acceptance criteria:
1. `LaneScheduler::check_starvation` reports starvation for a queued lane after the configured window even if the lane has never completed a task.
2. The scheduler records accurate starvation telemetry and audit evidence for that case.
3. Inline tests in `runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` and the existing conformance expectation in `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs` agree on the same behavior.
4. Validation uses `rch` for cargo-heavy checks only.","notes":"Implemented in `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs`:\n- added `first_queued_at_ms` to track when queued work began waiting\n- starvation now uses the queued-work baseline even before any completion history exists\n- completion resets the queued baseline when work remains queued\n- inline tests now cover pre-window no-starvation, queued-before-first-completion starvation, and audit evidence\n\nVerification status:\n- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo check --all-targets` PASS for `frankenengine-node`; dependency warnings remain in `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/control_plane_mock_inventory.rs`\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` blocked by pre-existing unrelated lints in `crates/franken-node/src/connector/canonical_serializer.rs` and `crates/franken-node/src/connector/crdt.rs`\n- targeted `rch` cargo test lanes are still running/stalling in the shared worker fleet; no green test verdict captured yet

 {"id":"bd-qrqs1","title":"Fresh-eyes audit of recent agent Rust fixes","description":"Review recent agent-written changes in crates/franken-node/src/supply_chain/certification.rs and crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs plus adjacent tests and call sites; diagnose any logic, reliability, or edge-case bugs from first principles; fix root causes; validate with rch-offloaded cargo checks/tests as needed; report findings to Agent Mail.","notes":"2026-03-12 audit pass findings:

- Patched crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs to latch starvation state, emit one LANE_STARVED event per starvation episode, and emit LANE_STARVATION_CLEARED on recovery while still returning current starvation status from check_starvation().
- Patched crates/franken-node/src/main.rs registry request builders to fail closed when provenance link signing fails and to derive provenance.vcs_commit_sha with a bounded prefix helper instead of panic-prone `[..12]` slicing on caller-provided hashes. Added a short-hash regression test.
